title: zygalchemilla pinnata
intro: Zygalchemilla pinnata, also known as Creeping Jenny or Spotted Cornelian, is a perennial flowering plant in the buckwheat family. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Zygalchemilla pinnata include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote digestive health. Herbal preparations of the plant include tea, tincture, and topical creams, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
